summary: Defined the dynamic localization formatting ownership contract without changing app source, localization resources, or translations.

contract_status_changes.before: The localization inventory deferred dynamic strings but did not define where counts, dates, notification copy, protocol statuses, or display-name adapters should live.
contract_status_changes.after: docs/workflows/localization-dynamic-formatting.md now defines the boundary that domain returns meaning, application coordinates runtime-owned messages, and SwiftUI/presentation code owns localized display formatting.

contract_status_changes.before: Future dynamic localization work could start with broad source changes and risk mixing UI copy into domain rules.
contract_status_changes.after: The workflow doc classifies implementation batches for Today plurals, digest formatting, protocol schedule/status projection, notification copy, and model display-name adapters, with owners, APIs, validation paths, risks, and out-of-scope items.

residual_risks: Dynamic/interpolated localization remains unimplemented; this slice intentionally only establishes the boundary and implementation plan.
Non-English locale values remain English placeholders and still require translation-quality review.
Running-app locale smoke, screenshot proof, device verification, and TestFlight verification remain deferred.
recommended_next_reason: Today dashboard counts and accessibility interpolation are high-visibility dynamic strings with a clear owner and narrow validation path.
